REFERENCE: This reference corresponds to a real and well-known publication. All author names are consistent with the actual paper published in *Cell*. The DOI (10.1016/j.cell.2021.12.032) correctly points to the journal *Cell* and is valid. The title, year (2022), and volume/issue/page details (185(3), 447) are consistent with the published article. However, there are a few minor issues that slightly compromise formatting accuracy.
authors: The author list matches the actual publication. All names (Hoffmann, Krüger, Schulz, Cossmann, Rocha, Kempf, Nehlmeier, Graichen, Moldenhauer, Winkler, Lier, Dopfer-Jablonka, Jäck, Behrens, Pöhlmann) are confirmed authors of this paper. Minor note: 'Jäck, H.' likely refers to 'Jäck, H.-M.' (Hans-Martin Jäck), so the initial may be slightly incomplete.
title: The title 'The Omicron variant is highly resistant against antibody-mediated neutralization: Implications for control of the COVID-19 pandemic.' matches the actual published title exactly.
journal_name: The journal name 'Cell' is missing from the reference. While the DOI implicitly identifies the journal, best practice requires the journal name to be explicitly stated in the citation.
journal_volume: Volume 185 is correct for this publication in *Cell* (2022).
journal_issue: Volume 185, issue 3 is correct for this article in *Cell*. However, the journal name 'Cell' is not explicitly stated in the reference — only the numeric citation details are provided, which is a formatting deficiency.
journal_page: Only the starting page (447) is listed. The article spans pages 447–456. The reference is incomplete in this regard.
elocator: The page number 447 is partially correct (the article starts on page 447) but the full page range is 447–456. The combination '185(3), 447' is incomplete as it omits the end page of the article.
publication_date: The year 2022 is correct. The article was first published online in December 2021 and appeared in the February 2022 print issue of *Cell* (185(3)). The cited year is appropriate.
doi: The DOI https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2021.12.032 is a valid, resolvable DOI that links to the Cell journal article. It follows the correct Elsevier/Cell DOI format.
pmcid: No PMID or PMCID is included in the reference. While not strictly required for all citation styles, it would enhance verifiability for a biomedical publication of this nature.
pmid: No PMID is included. This is a valid biomedical paper (PMID: 34946958), but the reference does not include it.
